behavior_summary: Implements a palace guardsman NPC's observable behaviors: how it appears when examined, how it responds to theft, area entry and periodic scheduling (used to revive/reset the NPC), and a small dialog tree when 'used' (talked to).
U8P_GUARD10::look describes the NPC as either "dead guardsman" or "guardsman" depending on its dead state and directs that short description at the observing process/player.

U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea is an area-trigger that, if the guard is dead, clears dead/in-combat state and teleports the NPC to spawn coordinates; otherwise it spawns a behavior method (likely an alert/approach routine).
U8P_GUARD10::schedule periodically spawns a scheduling method and, based on its result, can clear the 'dead' flag and spawn a revival/reinitialization method.

U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething checks the type of a stolen item against guarded types and, if guards haven't already been called (global guardsCalled false), barks 'Stop! Thief!' and spawns a guard-calling process.

U8P_GUARD10::use presents a small talk dialog to the player: chooses one of two intro lines randomly, builds a choice list (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, goodbye), branches on the answer and barks context-appropriate replies. A global flag devonInRule affects the 'Where am I?' response.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD10::look: branch on Npc::isDead(self) -> Item::bark(pid,self,"dead guardsman ") OR Item::bark(pid,self,"guardsman ").
U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea: if Npc::isDead(self) then Npc::clrDead(self), Npc::clrInCombat(self), Npc::teleport(self, 0x29,0x00,0x64,0x64) else spawn METHOD::15E7(pid,self).
U8P_GUARD10::schedule: spawn METHOD::143A; if its result indicates a particular state (zero/inactive) then call Npc::clrDead(self) and spawn METHOD::133F for reinitialization.

U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething: call Item::getType(item_pid) and compare against a set of guarded item type constants; if any match AND global guardsCalled is false then bark 'Stop! Thief!' and spawn CALGUARD::0080 to call other guards.

U8P_GUARD10::use: call urandom(100) to pick one of two starter lines, build an slist of dialog choices, call Item::ask(pid,slist,self) and branch on the returned string; devonInRule controls one branch's content.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings of the teleport parameters passed to Npc::teleport (map X/Y/orientation/level) and how they map to a spawn location.
Precise item type constants checked in AvatarStoleSomething (the exact type IDs and which in-game items they correspond to).

Detailed behavior of the spawned methods (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::0BCF, CALGUARD::0080, FREE::1C19, FREE::2A56): their internal logic is external to this file and only inferred from usage.
Whether guardsCalled is set/reset elsewhere in game code and the exact lifecycle intended for that throttle flag.
The intended frequency and triggering semantics of the schedule method (timing parameters are present but mapping to game time is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD10::look
main_function.purpose: When a player/process examines this guardsman NPC, present a short descriptive line that reflects whether the NPC is dead or alive and direct that line to the examining process.
entry_conditions: Event 0: the Avatar (or other actor) looks at / inspects this guard NPC (area-neutral U8P_GUARD10::look/examine interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that decides and emits a one-line description (alive vs dead) to the viewer.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-enter trigger: revives/teleports the guard when dead or spawns an approach/alert behavior when alive.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler: spawns timing methods and may clear dead state and reinitialize the guard based on results.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Responds to theft events: checks stolen item types, prevents repeat calling via guardsCalled, and alerts/spawns guard-response when needed.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::use
function_map.role: Talk/use interaction: randomizes an opening line, presents a dialog choice list to the player, and barks context-appropriate replies (uses devonInRule to vary one branch).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine/utility query to test if an NPC is flagged as dead.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short spoken/text line from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: Clear the NPC 'dead' flag (used when reviving/resetting).

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Clear the NPC 'in combat' flag (used during revive/reset).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport/place the NPC at specific coordinates/orientation (used for respawn/placement).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return the type/ID of an item (used to decide whether a stolen item should trigger guards).

function_map.name: Item::ask
function_map.role: Present a list of dialog options to the player and return the chosen string/index.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to randomize dialog selection.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Spawn/launch another process/method (used to call AI routines, guard calls, free callbacks, etc.).

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Framework free/callback used in dialog flow (inferred cleanup or delayed callback).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Framework free/callback used in dialog flow (inferred cleanup or delayed callback).

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the guard is dead to choose the correct description.
Emit the short description ('dead guardsman ' or 'guardsman ') to the examining process/player.
Decide whether to perform revive/teleport sequence or spawn an approach behavior.
Clear the dead flag as part of reviving/resetting the NPC.
Clear in-combat state as part of revive/reset.
Teleport the NPC to specific coordinates/orientation when reviving/resetting.
Spawn METHOD::15E7(pid,self) when an actor enters the fast-area and the guard is alive (likely an alert/approach routine).
Spawn periodic scheduling method METHOD::143A and later possibly METHOD::133F for revival based on result.
Clear dead flag if the schedule result indicates revival should occur.
Obtain the stolen item's type to check whether it is guarded and should trigger an alarm.
Shout 'Stop! Thief!' to the player/process when a guarded item is stolen and guards haven't yet been called.
Spawn CALGUARD::0080 to call other guards/respond to theft when appropriate.
Emit initial greeting/bark when the player uses/talks to the guard and for subsequent replies.
Randomly choose between two opening lines to make dialog less repetitive.
Present the short choice list (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, goodbye) and receive the player's selection.
Spawn FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 (free/callbacks) and METHOD::0BCF during dialog flow (cleanup/timing or auxiliary behaviors).

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Throttles or records whether guards have already been alerted/called to prevent repeated alarm behavior; read when deciding whether to bark and spawn guard-calling.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Alters dialog content in the 'Where am I?' branch of U8P_GUARD10::use; indicates presence/state of some rule or character 'Devon' that changes reply tone/content.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a palace guardsman NPC's observable behaviors: how it appears when examined, how it responds to theft, area entry and periodic scheduling (used to revive/reset the NPC), and a small dialog tree when 'used' (talked to).
